content_markdown: A satellite in close-Earth orbit moves to an orbit further from the Earth's surface. Which of the following concerning the speed of the satellite and its gravitational potential energy in the new orbit is correct?

diagram_metadata.description: A table showing the relationship between the speed of the satellite and its gravitational potential energy for four options A, B, C, and D.

options.label: A
options.text: Speed of the satellite: increases; Gravitational potential energy: decreases

options.label: B
options.text: Speed of the satellite: increases; Gravitational potential energy: increases

options.label: C
options.text: Speed of the satellite: decreases; Gravitational potential energy: decreases

options.label: D
options.text: Speed of the satellite: decreases; Gravitational potential energy: increases

vibe_explanation: As the orbital radius $r$ increases, the orbital speed $v = \sqrt{\frac{GM}{r}}$ decreases because the gravitational pull is weaker. Simultaneously, the gravitational potential energy $E_p = -\frac{GMm}{r}$ becomes less negative (closer to zero), which means it increases.
